Why I Gave Up Evernote for Notion

Notion is a clean, capable note-taking app — clean enough that I dropped Evernote after almost five years on it. This post is about why I made the switch — Evernote's pricing/sync/Markdown gaps versus Notion's blocks, Markdown support, templates, workspaces, and the education plan that gives students Notion Personal for free.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (9) - Moving Sprites With Speed (vx and vy)

This one's still about moving images around, but this time we're focusing on speed. PixiJS gives you vx and vy properties that let you control how fast and in which direction a sprite moves. We'll walk through how to use them, how to pass speed in as parameters, and how to make things move faster or change direction on the fly.
JavaScript Core Concepts (2): LHS vs RHS — What Those Errors Actually Mean

You'll run into LHS and RHS all over JavaScript — they're tied to assigning values and looking values up. This post breaks down what LHS and RHS mean, when each one throws an error, and why the exact wording of those errors changes from browser to browser.
How to Increase Your Google AdSense Revenue (Placement Tips)

A short notes post on the small things that move the needle on blog AdSense revenue — mostly about ad placement. Covers picking the right ad type (responsive Display ads), how many ads is too many, slots that are reliably visible (sidebar, article top, article bottom), and the two Google AdSense rules you should never break (no asking users to click, no clicking your own ads).
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (8) - Ticker, Round 2: The Rest of the API

Last time we finally got an image moving. This one's a bit more relaxed — we just walk through the rest of the ticker API we used to move that image: add, addOnce, destroy, remove, start, stop, and update. Short and sweet.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (7) - Ticker: Making Things Actually Move

We already know how to load an image onto the canvas — but a static image is kind of boring, right? So this time let's make it move. In this post I walk through PixiJS's ticker (the game loop), how it fires 60 times a second, and how to use a bit of if...else logic to bounce a sprite back and forth across the screen.
What Should You Put on Your Resume? Coursework or Portfolio?

As a career-switcher into engineering, there's one really common mistake juniors make — putting course assignments in their portfolio. Let's talk about why that's not the move, and what to do instead.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (6) - Texture (2): A Second Way to Load Images

Last time we rendered images with new PIXI.Loader(). This post covers another way to do the same thing — PIXI.Loader.shared — and then we have some fun loading an image and spawning 25 copies of it on screen, laid out in a grid with anchors and a little positioning math.
Console Is the Debug Tool You Already Have — Here's How to Actually Use It

When you're writing JavaScript, you reach for console to debug all the time. So this post takes a quick look at console — not just console.log, but the other methods like console.table, console.dir, console.time, and the warn/error/clear ones too. Handy stuff you'll probably use way more than you expect.
VSCode 1.43 Syntax Highlight Issue (and How to Fix It)
VSCode updated to 1.43 and suddenly syntax highlighting looks off. Not sure if it's a deliberate change or a bug, but here's how to restore the original highlighting.
